source_paper_title: Improved Bounds for Single-Nomination Impartial Selection
context:
Section 5 (Upper Bounds), end of the section introduction on page 14 (PDF p. 14 / 32), immediately after Corollary 5.2 is previewed

Like the bounds of Fischer and Klimm they approach 3/4 as n \(\to\) \(\infty\) and thus do not rule out the existence of an impartial mechanism that is close to 3/4-optimal when n is large. Whether such a mechanism exists is an intriguing open question, the resolution of which is likely to require new insights.
title: Existence of an impartial selection mechanism with asymptotic performance guarantee arbitrarily close to \(3/4\) in the single-nomination model
background_latex:
- For \(n\in\mathbb{N}\), let \(G_n\) be the set of directed graphs \(G=(V,E)\) with \(V=\{1,\dots,n\}\), no self-loops, and exactly one outgoing edge per vertex.
- For \(v\in V\), let \(\delta^-(v,G)=|\{u\in V:(u,v)\in E\}|\) be the indegree of \(v\). Let \(\Delta(G)=\max_{v\in V}\delta^-(v,G)\).
- A (randomized) selection mechanism is a map \(f:G_n\to[0,1]^n\) with \(\sum_{v\in V} f_v(G)=1\) for all \(G\in G_n\); it selects vertex \(v\) with probability \(f_v(G)\).
- The mechanism \(f\) is impartial if for any \(G=(V,E),G'=(V,E')\in G_n\) and any \(v\in V\), whenever \(E\setminus(\{v\}\times V)=E'\setminus(\{v\}\times V)\) (i.e., only \(v\)'s outgoing edge may differ), we have \(f_v(G)=f_v(G')\).
- For \(\alpha\le 1\), the mechanism \(f\) is \(\alpha\)-optimal on \(G_n\) if
\[\inf_{G\in G_n:\,\Delta(G)>0}\frac{\mathbb{E}_{v\sim f(G)}[\delta^-(v,G)]}{\Delta(G)}\ge \alpha.\]
- Let \(\alpha_n^*\) denote the supremum of \(\alpha\) such that there exists an impartial \(\alpha\)-optimal mechanism on \(G_n\).
problem_definition_latex:
Problem: Determine whether for every \(\varepsilon>0\) there exists \(N\in\mathbb{N}\) such that for all \(n\ge N\) there is an impartial selection mechanism \(f\) on \(G_n\) that is \((3/4-\varepsilon)\)-optimal, i.e.,
\[\inf_{G\in G_n:\,\Delta(G)>0}\frac{\mathbb{E}_{v\sim f(G)}[\delta^-(v,G)]}{\Delta(G)}\ge \frac{3}{4}-\varepsilon.\]
Equivalently, decide whether \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}\alpha_n^* = 3/4\) holds.

Problem:
Problem: Determine whether for every \(\varepsilon>0\) there exists \(N\in\mathbb{N}\) such that for all \(n\ge N\) there is an impartial selection mechanism \(f\) on \(G_n\) that is \((3/4-\varepsilon)\)-optimal, i.e.,
\[\inf_{G\in G_n:\,\Delta(G)>0}\frac{\mathbb{E}_{v\sim f(G)}[\delta^-(v,G)]}{\Delta(G)}\ge \frac{3}{4}-\varepsilon.\]
Equivalently, decide whether \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}\alpha_n^* = 3/4\) holds.
